name: Plitvice Lakes
description: A chain of 16 wondrous lakes formed as a result of the gradual dissolution of soluble layers of bedrock.

name: Blue Cave of Bisevo
description: An ethereal glowing blue ocean cave in Croatia.

name: Sea Organ
description: A musical instrument played by the sea.

name: Froggyland
description: A wonderfully strange collection of 500+ stuffed frogs doing people things.

name: Museum of Broken Relationships
description: Where the lovelorn find a home for their sentimental keepsakes.

name: Zeljava Airbase
description: Yugoslavia's biggest underground airport lies in ruins.

name: Podgaric Monument
description: This stunning, out-of-the-way Croatian monument commemorates a community's uprising against occupying fascist forces.

name: Abandoned Haludovo Palace Hotel
description: This truly palatial work of Communist-era architecture now sits crumbling on a Croatian beach.

name: St. Nicholas Fortress
description: Large, airy hallways hide within an impenetrable fortress that beckons urban explorers.

name: Ružica Grad
description: Legend has it fairies gathered at the site of this medieval castle.

name: Dubrovnik Maskeron
description: Gargoyle head that brings good luck.

name: Jablanac Shipwreck
description: The remains of a sunken military ship wreck visible through clear water.

name: UNESCO-Protected SPAR Supermarket
description: When a supermarket opened up shop within a medieval building it became protected by UNESCO on a technicality.

name: Brestovac Sanatorium
description: Once a state-of-the-art tuberculosis treatment spa, these ruins are now ruled by paintball players.

name: Dubrovnik Cathedral Treasury (Riznica Katedrale)
description: A wunderkammer of gold and silver reliquaries and ancient saintly bones.

name: Visovac Monastery
description: This picturesque medieval monastery guards a small trove of historic treasures.

name: Petrova Gora
description: This huge metal monument is slowly being stripped to its concrete skeleton.

name: Pula Arena
description: One of the best-preserved Roman amphitheaters is still used today, but for less brutal events.

name: Tesla's Birthplace Museum
description: Humble beginnings for one of the world's most famous scientists.

name: The Abandoned Hotels of Kupari
description: The now-overgrown beach resort was once frequented by Yugoslav military elite.

name: Villa Rebar
description: This former estate of a fascist leader is now a burnt out ruin leading to a system of secret tunnels.

name: Trakošćan Castle
description: This Croatian fairytale castle is slowly falling apart thanks to years of neglect.

name: Walls of Ston
description: Longest fortress system anywhere in Europe.

name: Nine Views
description: This scale model of our solar system hid in plain sight for years before a physics team sought out all the planets.

name: The Relic of St. Silvan
description: A full figure 4th century incorruptible saint lies in a glass coffin in Croatia.

name: Pietrapelosa
description: Croatia's "hairy stone" fortress is one of the most well preserved Istrian castles.

name: Torpedo Launch Station
description: Now abandoned, the world's first torpedo factory is crumbling into the sea.

name: Croatian Museum of Naïve Art
description: An eclectic art form practiced by untrained masters is celebrated at this museum in the heart of Zagreb's old Upper Town.

name: Stone Flower
description: Croatia's towering concrete blossom grows from the site of a World War II concentration camp.

name: Vis Island
description: Formerly Yugoslavia's "forbidden island," this sleepy little city still holds the abandoned remains of military strongholds.

name: Trsteno Arboretum
description: The oldest arboretum on the Dalmatian coast doubles as the gardens of Kings Landing in "Game of Thrones."

name: Abandoned Tunnels of Korčula
description: When Croatia became independent of Yugoslavia, the splintering governments left behind these concrete tunnels.

name: Monument to the Detachment in Brezovica Forest
description: A tower marking the spot where Yugoslavia began its resistance against the invading Nazis stands neglected, but not quite forgotten.

name: Goli Otok
description: The only gulag ever to exist in socialist Yugoslavia.

name: Muzej Mamurluka (Museum of Hangovers)
description: A peculiar museum that includes many drunken tales, a drunk driving simulator, and much more.

name: Edivo Vina Winery
description: Dive for drinks at Croatia's first and only underwater winery.

name: Gregory of Nin’s Big Toe
description: The big toe of this towering monument to a Croatian national hero is worn from years of superstitious rubbing.

name: Rijeka Tunnel
description: This military tunnel dates back to World War II and provides visitors with a little wartime adrenaline.

name: Orlando's Column in Dubrovnik
description: An ancient knight and a symbol of the city's freedom.

name: Daksa Island
description: This Croatian island is for sale but no one is buying due to the dozens of accused Nazi sympathizers who were massacred there after WWII.

name: Museum of Mushrooms
description: This only museum in the world dedicated solely to mushrooms.

name: Pillar of Shame
description: Now harmless, this looming post struck fear into the heart of medieval troublemakers.

name: Grič Tunnel
description: A World War II evacuation tunnel turned into a history display of Croatian state and people.

name: Liberland
description: A self-proclaimed libertarian micro-state on a tiny bit of disputed territory between Serbia and Croatia.

name: Pag Triangle
description: Just a few miles from Novalja, a strange geological oddity has left scientists baffled.

name: Zrinjevac Meteorological Column
description: This 135-year-old weather station studied the effects of the climate on human health.

name: Museum Of Modern Art Dubrovnik (MoMAD)
description: Inside this mansion turned museum, the works of prominent Croatian artists are on full display.

name: Veliki Tabor Castle
description: This beautiful Croatian fortress is haunted by legends of forbidden love.

name: The Red Fićo
description: A tribute to an iconic act of defiance from the Croatian War of Independence involving a small car.

name: Dudik Memorial Park
description: Built as an anti-fascist tribute, this surreal Yugoslav memorial became an unwelcome symbol after the Croatian war for independence.

name: Sea Tunnel at St. Anthony’s Canal
description: This unique structure allowed boats to bypass underwater mine barriers in the channel to enter the harbor.

name: Brod Fortress
description: This abandoned Croatian stronghold never saw a great deal of conflict but it is still a beloved relic nonetheless.

name: Sebastian Art Gallery
description: This 15th-century church houses priceless works of art from a range of Croatian artists.

name: Grožnjan Town Loggia
description: The Renaissance-era courthouse is a relic from the fortified town's centuries of Venetian rule.

name: PEEK&POKE Computer Museum
description: This Croatian computer club features such gaming relics as the infamous Virtual Boy and an 8-bit Pacman machine.

name: Brioni
description: Island wonderland for communist autocrats.

name: Galešnjak Island
description: This heart-shaped island looks substantially less lovely after a recent bulldozing project.

name: The Museum of Lost Tales
description: A fantastical display of Croatian folklore, mythology, and myths.

name: Ludbreg's 'Center of the World'
description: Colorful concentric circles honor a small Croatian town's supposed spot at the nucleus of it all.

name: Red History Museum
description: A unique interactive experience of everyday life in communist Yugoslavia—a very different time in Croatian history.

name: Svetište Sv. Marije na Škriljinah (The Sanctuary of St. Mary of the Rocks)
description: Inside this small chapel tucked away in the forest is one of the most well-preserved Danse Macabre frescos.

name: Muzej Krapinskih Neandertalaca (Krapina Neanderthal Museum)
description: This modern, multimedia museum takes visitors on an evolutionary journey back in time.

name: Limski Kanal
description: This narrow fjord-like bay is the largest ria in the Mediterranean Sea.

name: Tounj Bridge
description: A unique two-level bridge designed to honor a king.

name: Varaždin Cemetery
description: Trimmed trees grow alongside the graves like topiary tombstones.

name: Fontana s Kornjačama (Fountain with Turtles)
description: This fountain has a growing (animal) population, and might also be growing in size.

name: Dioklecijanov Akvadukt (Diocletian Aqueduct)
description: This ancient Roman aqueduct once funneled water to Diocletian's palace.

name: Verdis
description: A small self-proclaimed micro-state located on a piece of disputed territory between Croatia and Serbia, near Liberland.

name: Crkva Sv. Martina (St. Martin's Church)
description: Split's smallest church, and one of its oldest, has been reconstructed inside the city's Roman walls.

name: Abandoned Village of Malo Grablje
description: This deserted town in the hills of Croatia’s "party island" may have a scandalous connection to King Henry VIII.

name: Zagreb 80s Museum
description: A house that transports visitors back to Yugoslavia.

name: Mačkamama
description: A statue of Paulina Hermann, the eccentric noblewoman known as the "mother of cats."

name: Murals of Vodnjan
description: Maze-like streets filled with vibrant murals draw visitors to this Istrian town.

name: Old Town Walls Basketball Courts
description: Picturesque basketball courts sheltered by historical defensive walls and with an enviable sea view.

name: Zadarski Barkajoli
description: The medieval ferry service has been going strong since the 14th century.

name: Crkva Sv. Vida (Church of St. Vitus)
description: One of the very few mosques built by the Ottomans still standing in Croatia is now a Catholic church.

name: Kamenita Vrata (Our Lady of the Stone Gate)
description: A well-worshipped shrine to the Virgin Mary nestled inside the last of Zagreb’s historic gates.

name: Igralište Batarija
description: The local club's soccer field is practically surrounded by UNESCO-protected monuments.

name: Kažnjavanje Dirke (The Punishment of Dirce)
description: A Roman mosaic depicting a classical myth was only rediscovered after bombing during World War II.

name: Cat Caffe Zagreb
description: A safe sanctuary for rescued shelter cats with an imaginative, glitter-infused experience

name: Museum of Illusions
description: The original location of a global franchise in Zagreb stands out for exhibits that challenge perceptions.

name: Crkva Sv. Sebastijana (Church of St. Sebastian)
description: The interior of this church has been turned into a memorial to locals who died in Croatia's Homeland War.

name: Krstionica Sv. Trojstva (Baptismal Font of the Holy Spirit)
description: This seven-sided Romanesque chapel is regarded as the oldest building in Rovinj.

name: Quarter of Saint Theodore
description: An excavation site that signified Hercules in Pula, and a glimpse into the city's Roman history.

name: Partisan and Victims of Fascism Memorial
description: A memorial dedicated to the 29,000 Istrian freedom fighters of World War II.

name: Luka Mozaika
description: This work of art is considered the world's longest mosaic.

name: Sveta Srca
description: A former church turned museum, complete with changing exhibitions.

name: Church of the Holy Cross
description: The “world’s smallest cathedral” is a remnant of one of Croatia’s medieval royal towns.

name: Pelješac Bridge
description: Construction of this bridge was once described as an "act of violence" against a neighboring country.

name: Fort Forno
description: A strategically positioned fortress that protected the Fazana channel has found new life as a place for theater.

name: The Bridge Over Foša (Pješački most)
description: This pedestrian bridge in Croatia is a sleek, ship-inspired design with a fun net and social spaces.

name: Teleferika
description: The remains of a cable car used for bauxite mining after World War I hides in a coastal forest.

name: Dioš Castle
description: Built in 1904 as a lavish gift for a daughter, it is one of Croatia's youngest and most architecturally unique castles, blending Art Nouveau with neo-Gothic elements.

name: Siscia in Situ
description: One of the major cities of Late Roman provinces, now in ruins of small scale.

name: Joakim Rakovac Memorial
description: A monument to one of Croatia's famous freedom fighters.

name: Majsan Armored Personnel Carrier
description: This armored vehicle was built in the local shipyard for use by the defenders of Dubrovnik.

name: Barutana Memorial Area
description: Site of a massive 1991 ammunition depot explosion that marked a turning point in the Croatian War of Independence.

name: CROATA Museum Concept Store
description: This silk-filled boutique is housed in a 12th-century palace and offers a rare blend of craftsmanship, culture, and quiet elegance.

name: Hotel Belvedere
description: The complex has been abandoned since the Croatian War of Independence, barring a stint as a set for 'Game of Thrones.'

name: Korenica Monument
description: A forgotten monument to Yugoslavian freedom.
